growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MS) with atrazine as sole source of C, N and energy

up.method: Michaelis-Menten fit (Eq.3) to experimentally measured qs at each steady-state D; qs_max = 4.08 +/- 0.51 g S (g X)-1 h-1, Ks = 237 +/- 58 ug/L (~1 uM)
conversion of qs_max 4.08 g/gDW/h using atrazine MW 215.68 g/mol: 4.08/215.68 = 0.01892 mol/gDW/h = 18.9 mmol/gDW/h

sec.units: metabolite (concentration reported vs D, not a rate)
sec.method: HPLC; 2-OH-atrazine and cyanuric acid formed; concentrations decreased at lower D; no numeric secretion rate given

yields.Yxs: no single numeric value in main text (shown vs D in Fig.1c; no significant difference across D); Yxs_max fitted via Herbert-Pirt Eq.4 (denoted Y^G)

growth_records.cite: Kankana Kundu et al. Defining lower limits of biodegradation: atrazine degradation regulated by mass transfer and maintenance demand in Arthrobacter aurescens TC1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0430-z. [PMC6776027]
growth_records.snippet: Equation 3 was fitted using measured qs and Cs to estimate qs_max and Ks as 4.08 +/- 0.51 g S (g X)-1 h-1 and 237 +/- 58 ug/L (~1 uM), respectively. ... The resultant ms was 0.25 g S (g X)-1 h-1, which translates into mE = 97 +/- 10 kJ (C-m
growth_records.notes: Aggregate chemostat record. At steady state mu = D for each of the 8 dilution rates (0.006-0.068 h-1). Doubling time td = ln2/mu: at D=0.068, td=ln2/0.068=10.19 h; at D=0.006, td=ln2/0.006=115.5 h. Maintenance coefficient ms = 0.25 g S (g X)-1 h-1 (fit of atrazine conc. vs D to modified Herbert-Pirt Eq.4). mE = 97 +/- 10 kJ (C-mol biomass)-1 h-1 based on delta_R G0'cat = -3338 kJ/mol. Cell conc 2.2-2.69e7 cells/mL (D high-medium), 1.31e7 cells/mL (D low 0.006-0.009), viability ~90%. Per-D individual measured qs values are in Supplementary Table S1 (not present in article XML). qs_max unit conversion: atrazine C8H14ClN5 MW = 215.68 g/mol -> 4.08 g/gDW/h / 215.68 = 18.9 mmol/gDW/h. Onset of mass transfer limitation at residual 30 ug/L atrazine, population td=14 days.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MS) with atrazine as sole source of C, N and energy

up.method: van Verseveld biomass model (Eq.5) fit to retentostat biomass accumulation; ms = 0.11 g S (g X)-1 h-1 (collinearity index 1.30)

mfa.note: disappearance of isotope fractionation (-0.45 +/- 0.36 permil, statistically indistinct from 0) indicates complete mass transfer limitation at near-zero growth; not a carbon flux per se but the isotope-based observable

yields.Yxs: Yxs_max estimated jointly with ms via van Verseveld Eq.5 (no standalone numeric in main text)

growth_records.cite: Kankana Kundu et al. Defining lower limits of biodegradation: atrazine degradation regulated by mass transfer and maintenance demand in Arthrobacter aurescens TC1. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0430-z. [PMC6776027]
growth_records.snippet: The estimated ms was 0.11 g S (g X)-1 h-1 corresponding to mE = 37 +/- 8 kJ (Cmol biomass)-1 h-1, which is ~2 times lower than in chemostats. ... mu decreased from 0.018 to 0.005 h-1 and, finally, reached a level of 0.0001 h-1 after 42 days
growth_records.notes: Retentostat at D=0.02 h-1. Specific growth rate mu declined during cultivation from 0.018 -> 0.005 -> final 0.0001 h-1 (near-zero growth). Doubling time at final mu: td = ln2/0.0001 = 6931 h = 289 days (paper reports td=225 days from van Verseveld fit in main text; abstract states td=265 days at 12 +/- 3 ug/L residual atrazine - modest internal discrepancy; I record final mu=0.0001 h-1 and paper-stated td=225 days -> 225 d x 24 = 5400 h). Maintenance coefficient ms = 0.11 g S (g X)-1 h-1 (~2x lower than chemostat 0.25); mE = 37 +/- 8 kJ (C-mol biomass)-1 h-1. Peak cell conc 2.79e8 cells/mL at end of growth phase; viability 99.5 +/- 0.5%. 97% of energy flux directed to maintenance. eps 13C = -0.45 +/- 0.36 permil (mass-transfer limited).
